concept.conceptName: Oxygen saturation Calculated from oxygen partial pressure in Arterial blood
Oxygen saturation [Pure mass fraction] in Arterial blood
Oxygen saturation [Pure mass fraction] Calculated from oxygen partial pressure in Arterial blood

en.name: Oxygen saturation in arterial blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Arterial oxygen saturation (SaO2) is the fraction of haemoglobin in arterial blood bound to oxygen, expressed as a percentage. It is measured on an arterial blood sample by a CO-oximeter (multi-wavelength spectrophotometer in the blood gas analyser), which directly distinguishes oxyhaemoglobin from deoxy-, carboxy- and methaemoglobin [1]. SaO2 is the reference for arterial oxygenation and reflects the position on the haemoglobin-oxygen dissociation curve at the patient's PaO2. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, reported as a percentage (0-100%). **Note: SaO2 vs SpO2.** This concept set covers **SaO2 measured by CO-oximetry on an arterial blood sample only** — a one-shot laboratory measurement. It does **not** cover pulse oximetry (SpO2), which is a continuous, transcutaneous estimate produced by a pulse oximeter at the fingertip or earlobe. SpO2 is biased upward of SaO2 in carbon monoxide intoxication, methemoglobinemia, severe anaemia or low perfusion, and is captured by a separate concept set ("Oxygen saturation by pulse oximetry", if defined; LOINC class is `Clinical Observation`). **Note: arterial vs venous.** Arterial saturation (SaO2 ~ 95-100% in healthy adults at sea level) and venous saturation (SvO2 ~ 65-75%) are different measurands and must not be merged. Venous and central venous saturation are captured by the separate concept set "Oxygen saturation in venous blood". **Note: directly measured vs calculated.** LOINC distinguishes `Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood` (direct CO-oximetry, code 2708-6) from `Oxygen saturation Calculated from oxygen partial pressure in Arterial blood` (computed from PaO2 via the haemoglobin dissociation curve, code 51733-4). Both forms are included here; the calculated variant is less reliable when dyshaemoglobinaemias are present. ## Included Concepts The set lists four LOINC "Lab Test" concepts for SaO2 (cord blood excluded), grouped by representation. ### Volume fraction (%, traditional) - *3016502 / 2708-6 — Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: most widely used. - *3039426 / 51733-4 — Oxygen saturation Calculated from oxygen partial pressure in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: derived from PaO2. ### Pure mass fraction (UCUM variants) - *42869607 / 71852-8 — Oxygen saturation [Pure mass fraction] in Arterial blood (LOINC)* - *42869598 / 71843-7 — Oxygen saturation [Pure mass fraction] Calculated from oxygen partial pressure in Arterial blood (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`%`** (concept 8554 — percent). The `[Pure mass fraction]` variants are dimensionless ratios between 0 and 1. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts that exist for arterial oxygen saturation are deliberately not part of this set, grouped by reason for exclusion. ### Pulse oximetry (SpO2) — different measurement principle - All `Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry` codes (LOINC class is mostly `Clinical Observation`): different measurement principle (transcutaneous, non-invasive, continuous), with different biases. ### Cord blood — physiologically distinct - *3014295 / 28642-7 — Oxygen saturation in Arterial cord blood* - *42869603 / 71848-6 — Oxygen saturation [Pure mass fraction] in Arterial cord blood* ### Other specimens - All venous, mixed venous, central venous codes: captured by the separate "Oxygen saturation in venous blood" concept set. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports SaO2 measured on an arterial blood gas (CO-oximetry): `3016502 / 2708-6 — Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood`. - **Source is a pulse oximetry reading (SpO2)**: do **not** map to this concept set. Use the dedicated pulse oximetry concept set. - **Source explicitly says "calculated from PaO2"**: map to the `Calculated from oxygen partial pressure` variant. - **Source reports SvO2 / ScvO2 (venous saturation)**: do **not** map. Use "Oxygen saturation in venous blood" instead. - **Do not confuse with**: SpO2 (pulse oximetry, different concept set), SvO2/ScvO2 (venous saturation, separate concept set), PaO2 (oxygen partial pressure, separate concept set). ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2708-6 — Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood. Available: https://loinc.org/2708-6/
